sections.paragraph: the seventeenth letter of the Hebrew alphabet, used as a numeral for eighty. The name of this letter probably signified a mouth, i.q. פֶּה. As to its pronunciation, see Lehrg. pp. 20, 21, where there is a refutation of the opinion of those who hold that פ, when dageshed, was not pronounced p by the Hebrews. It is interchanged principally with the other labials, ב and מ, which see.
